# Covenant

> Covenant is an online estate-planning service for expats in the United Arab Emirates. It helps people create and register a UAE will (through the ADJD or DIFC), add a coordinated home-country will, set up power of attorney, appoint guardians for children, and store documents securely.

Covenant is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.

## Key facts
- Service area: United Arab Emirates (all seven emirates).
- For: expats and cross-border families, including non-Muslims under Federal Decree-Law No. 41 of 2022.
- Registration routes: ADJD (Abu Dhabi Judicial Department, fully online) and DIFC Wills Service Centre.
- Pricing: UAE will from AED 799 (+ government fee); mirror wills for couples; add-ons for home-country wills and power of attorney.

## Guides
- [Guides / Blog](https://covenant.ae/blog): Articles on UAE wills, inheritance law, guardianship and estate planning for expats.

## Key pages
- [How it works](https://covenant.ae/#how-it-works)
- [Pricing](https://covenant.ae/#pricing)
